role: First Deputy Speaker of Parliament, 6th Majlis (2000–2004); Secretary-General, Islamic Iran Participation Front
bio: Mohammad-Reza Khatami is a nephrologist and reformist politician, the younger brother of [President Mohammad Khatami](/people/mohammad-khatami). He entered politics after his brother's 1997 victory, becoming the top-polling candidate in Tehran in the March 2000 election. He served as Deputy Speaker of the reformist 6th [Majlis](/orgs/islamic-consultative-assembly) alternating with [Behzad Nabavi](/people/behzad-nabavi). He founded and served as Secretary-General of the [Islamic Iran Participation Front](/orgs/islamic-iran-participation-front), Iran's largest reformist party. He is married to Zahra Eshraghi, a granddaughter of [Ruhollah Khomeini](/people/ruhollah-khomeini). He was permanently disqualified from running for office by the [Guardian Council](/orgs/guardian-council).
editorial_notes: Born 1959. Top-polling candidate in Tehran (March 2000) with 1,794,365 votes. Married Zahra Eshraghi (Khomeini's granddaughter) in 1983. Disqualified from all future office by Guardian Council.
